verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (HSP60) is canonically a mitochondrial chaperonin, but is well-documented to translocate to the plasma membrane outer leaflet under stress, oncogenic transformation, and apoptosis/ICD conditions. Cancer cells display HSP60 prominently on the cell surface (ecto-HSP60), where it has been exploited as a CAR-T and antibody target. In non-cancerous cells, stress and apoptosis also drive surface translocation. The protein lacks a classical TM domain or GPI anchor; surface display is cell-state-induced. This qualifies as contextual/cell_state_induced.
